check.command: sh -c 'cfg=${PGDATA?}/postgresql.conf; grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*pgaudit\.log_catalog[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*'"'"'on'"'"'' "$cfg" && grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*pgaudit\.log_level[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*'"'"'log'"'"'' "$cfg" && grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*pgaudit\.log_parameter[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*'"'"'on'"'"'' "$cfg" && grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*pgaudit\.log_statement_once[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*'"'"'off'"'"'' "$cfg" && grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*pgaudit\.log[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*'"'"'all,[[:space:]]*-misc'"'"'' "$cfg" && grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*log_line_prefix[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*'"'"'<[[:space:]]*%m[[:space:]]+%u[[:space:]]+%d[[:space:]]+%e:[[:space:]]*>'"'"'' "$cfg" && grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*log_error_verbosity[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*default' "$cfg" && printf Compliant'

candidate_check.description: PostgreSQL must produce audit records containing sufficient information to establish the outcome (success or failure) of the events.

fix_text_excerpt:
Using pgaudit PostgreSQL can be configured to audit various facets of PostgreSQL. See supplementary content APPENDIX-B for documentation on installing pgaudit. 

All errors, denials, and unsuccessful requests are logged if logging is enabled. See supplementary content APPENDIX-C for documentation on enabling logging. 

Note: The following instructions use the PGDATA and PGVER environment variables. See supplementary content APPENDIX-F for instructions on configuring PGDATA and APPENDIX-H for PGVER.

With pgaudit and logging enabled, set the following configuration settings in postgresql.conf, as the database administrator (shown here as "postgres"), to the following: 

$ sudo su - postgres
$ vi ${PGDATA?}/postgresql.conf 
pgaudit.log_catalog='on' 
pgaudit.log_level='log' 
pgaudit.log_parameter='on' 
pgaudit.log_statement_once='off' 
pgaudit.log='all, -misc' 

Next, tune the following logging configurations in postgresql.conf: 

$ sudo su - postgres
$ vi ${PGDATA?}/postgresql.conf 
log_line_prefix = '< %m %u %d %e: >' 
log_error_verbosity = default 

Last, as the system administrator, restart PostgreSQL: 

$ sudo systemctl reload postgresql-${PGVER?}
title: PostgreSQL must produce audit records containing sufficient information to establish the outcome (success or failure) of the events.
